title: Security vulnerabilities in transitive dependencies (brace-expansion and inflight)
author: MahogaWeerasinghe
body:
Hi team,

Security scanning tools (Snyk) are reporting vulnerabilities in transitive dependencies of ExcelJS 4.4.0.

1. brace-expansion@1.1.11 (via archiver → archiver-utils → glob → minimatch)

- Vulnerability: Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS)
- Severity: Low
- Snyk ID: SNYK-JS-BRACEEXPANSION-9789073
- Fixed in: 1.1.12, 2.0.2, 3.0.1, 4.0.1
- Link: https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JS-BRACEEXPANSION-9789073

2. inflight@1.0.6 (via archiver → archiver-utils → glob)

- Vulnerability: Missing Release of Resource after Effective Lifetime
- Severity: Medium
- Snyk ID: SNYK-JS-INFLIGHT-6095116
- Link: https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JS-INFLIGHT-6095116

**Dependency Chain**

exceljs@4.4.0
└── archiver@5.3.2
    └── archiver-utils@2.1.0
        └── glob@7.2.3
            ├── minimatch@3.1.2
            │   └── brace-expansion@1.1.11 ⚠️
            └── inflight@1.0.6 ⚠️

**Snyk Test Output**

```
Issues with no direct upgrade or patch:
  ✗ Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) [Low Severity] in brace-expansion@2.0.1
    introduced by exceljs@4.4.0 > archiver@5.3.2 > archiver-utils@2.1.0 > glob@7.2.3 > minimatch@3.1.2 > brace-expansion@1.1.11
  ✗ Missing Release of Resource after Effective Lifetime [Medium Severity] in inflight@1.0.6
    introduced by exceljs@4.4.0 > archiver@5.3.2 > archiver-utils@2.1.0 > glob@7.2.3 > inflight@1.0.6
```

Can you update the archiver dependency to a newer version that includes updated versions of glob and its transitive dependencies ?

I understand that dependency updates can sometimes introduce breaking changes or require testing, so no pressure at all. I just wanted to bring this to your attention in case it's something that could be addressed in a future release.

TIA

comments.author: nickiannone-fis
comments.body:
+1, also seeing a transitive vulnerability in inflight@1.0.6 reported by Snyk via Checkmarx.

Not seeing the same one with brace-expansion@1.1.11, because another dependency I'm using (ejs@3.1.10) pulls in brace-expansion@2.0.2 as a transitive dependency, so that version may be appropriate to resolve the issue. Another package I'm using pulls in brace-expansion@1.1.12, so that might work too. Worth testing.

comments.author: nickiannone-fis
comments.body: I'm looking into whether we can fix this just by bumping `archiver`. That package is at 7.0.1 now, and that build looks clean.

comments.author: tiagojufr
comments.body: `brace-expansion@<5` is affected by CVE-2026-25547. `exceljs@4.4.0` depends on it through `archiver@5.3.2`. Can we upgrade the `archiver` dependency please?

comments.author: gameroman
comments.body:
Hi, I've forked this into https://github.com/excel-js/exceljs if someone is interested

There is also https://github.com/cjnoname/excelts
